meta.title: Header
meta.description: Site header with navigation bar, logo, search, external link, and dropdown submenus. Single rounded bar on desktop with hamburger mobile menu.

tsx: @wordpress/components (PanelBody, BaseControl, ToggleControl, Button, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarButton, SelectControl)

requiredPatterns.usage: Maintain section order in parent Header block
Add settings shortcut to Header block toolbar
Logo rendering with inline SVG support
Logo upload when no logo is set
Sync block name in list view with link title
NEW: Must add to states.css for collapsed mobile menu
Editor scroll preview for fixed header behavior
Editor mobile detection for menu toggle
Editable text fields (search placeholder, mobile CTA text)

componentStructure.wrapper: div (NOT header -- lives inside WP template part <header>)

qualityChecklist: No content attributes on parent -- purely container
Sidebar controls with conditional hideOnScrollDown toggle
Scroll behavior CSS classes synced TSX/PHP
Editor useEffect for iframe scroll preview
inert attribute on hidden menu items
TSX and PHP produce identical HTML structure

blockHierarchy.description: Three-level inner block hierarchy. The Header is a simple container; HeaderMain is the single section that contains the nav bar with logo, utility row, nav items, and mobile menu. Each nav item (HeaderMainItem) can optionally contain sub-items (HeaderMainSubItem).
blockHierarchy.structure:
Header (container)
  └── HeaderMain (nav bar section with logo, search, SEP link, nav, mobile menu)
        └── HeaderMainItem (nav link with optional submenu)
              └── HeaderMainSubItem (submenu link)
notes: Unlike the typical header pattern with separate QuickAccess and Main sections, this design has a SINGLE rounded nav bar containing everything (logo, utility row, main nav) in one bar.

The SEP (Student Engagement Platform) button is NOT a separate section -- it's an attribute of HeaderMain that renders differently on desktop (text link in utility row) vs mobile (pill button above nav bar).
The mobile CTA footer (text + Contact Us button) is also an attribute of HeaderMain, rendered only inside the mobile menu panel.
Search is a decorative placeholder linking to a search page -- NOT an actual form input. It's an attribute on HeaderMain.
No HeaderQuickAccess section is needed. The utility row (search, separator, SEP link) is part of the HeaderMain right column.

attributes.name: anchor
attributes.description: HTML anchor (used for aria-controls on mobile menu)

attributes.name: logoId
attributes.description: Media library ID for the logo image/SVG

attributes.name: sepLink
attributes.description: Student Engagement Platform external link. On desktop: text link in utility row. On mobile: pill button above nav bar.
attributes.validation: shouldDisplay() -- hide entire SEP element when empty

attributes.name: searchLink
attributes.description: Search link. Renders as a styled link (not an actual search form). Links to the search page. Desktop: 250px in utility row. Mobile: full-width in menu panel.
attributes.validation: shouldDisplay() -- hide search when URL is empty

attributes.name: mobileCtaText
attributes.description: Text shown above the CTA button in mobile menu footer. e.g. 'Have an idea for a service or way we can better serve you?'

attributes.name: mobileCtaLink
attributes.description: CTA button in mobile menu footer. e.g. 'Contact Us'. Uses ThemeButton.
attributes.validation: shouldDisplay() -- hide entire mobile footer section when both mobileCtaText and mobileCtaLink are empty

attributes.name: fixedOnScrollDesktop
attributes.description: Whether the header becomes fixed (sticky) at the top when scrolling on desktop
attributes.editorComponent: ToggleControl in InspectorControls > Behaviour panel > Desktop

attributes.name: hideOnScrollDownDesktop
attributes.description: Whether the header hides when scrolling down (reveals on scroll up) on desktop. Only relevant when fixedOnScrollDesktop is true.
attributes.validation: Reset to false when fixedOnScrollDesktop is toggled off

attributes.name: fixedOnScrollMobile
attributes.description: Whether the header becomes fixed (sticky) at the top when scrolling on mobile
attributes.editorComponent: ToggleControl in InspectorControls > Behaviour panel > Mobile

attributes.name: hideOnScrollDownMobile
attributes.description: Whether the header hides when scrolling down on mobile. Only relevant when fixedOnScrollMobile is true.
attributes.validation: Reset to false when fixedOnScrollMobile is toggled off

attributes.name: link
attributes.description: Navigation link for this menu item

attributes.name: submenuAlignment
attributes.description: Desktop submenu dropdown alignment relative to parent item. 'left' aligns dropdown left edge with item. 'right' aligns dropdown right edge with item. Additionally, CSS anchor positioning with @position-try is used to prevent viewport overflow.

Header_wrapper.notes: Pure container. No visual styling. Lives inside WP template part <header>.

Header_inner.notes: Inner blocks wrapper. No additional classes needed.

HeaderMain_outerWrapper.classes: Contains the SEP mobile button (above) and the nav bar (below). flex flex-col gap-2 on mobile.
HeaderMain_outerWrapper.notes: This is the block root. On mobile, the SEP button and nav bar are stacked with 8px gap.

HeaderMain_sepButtonMobile.classes: md:hidden flex items-center justify-center gap-3 bg-charcoal text-white border border-charcoal rounded-full p-4 no-underline! font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] uppercase
HeaderMain_sepButtonMobile.notes: Mobile-only pill button for SEP external link. Hidden on desktop (md:hidden). Content: link title + external arrow SVG icon. Only rendered when sepLink has URL.

HeaderMain_navBar.classes: header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full

conditionalClasses.fixedOnScrollDesktop: See Section 3.5 of header.md block-type doc for full scroll behavior conditional classes

HeaderMain_navBar.notes: The main navigation bar. Charcoal background with 25px rounded corners. Contains logo, utility row (desktop), nav items, mobile toggle.

HeaderMain_navBarInner.notes: Flex container for logo and right column.

HeaderMain_logo.notes: Logo link to home page. Desktop height ~57px (h-14), mobile height ~26px (h-6.5). Uses ImageOrInlineSvg / theme_output_svg_or_img. The logo icon and text wordmark are a single SVG, with the gap baked in.

HeaderMain_rightColumn.classes: hidden md:flex flex-col gap-6 items-end shrink
HeaderMain_rightColumn.notes: Desktop only (hidden on mobile). Contains utility row on top, nav items row on bottom. gap-6 = 24px matches Figma.

HeaderMain_utilityRow.notes: Contains search link, vertical separator, SEP text link. gap-4 = 16px matches Figma.

HeaderMain_searchLink.classes: Desktop: w-[250px] border-b border-white pb-1 flex gap-2 items-center no-underline! group. Mobile (inside menu panel): w-full border-b border-white pb-2 flex gap-2 items-center no-underline!
HeaderMain_searchLink.notes: Search link styled to look like an input. NOT a form/input element. Links to search page. Desktop: 250px wide, pb-1 (4px). Mobile: full-width, pb-2 (8px).

children.text: font-sans font-medium text-sm leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase

HeaderMain_verticalSeparator.notes: Thin vertical line between search and SEP link. Desktop only. ~21px tall rotated line in Figma, simplified to a 1px wide, 20px tall vertical element.

HeaderMain_sepLinkDesktop.classes: hidden md:flex items-center gap-2 no-underline! font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase
HeaderMain_sepLinkDesktop.notes: Desktop text link for SEP. Hidden on mobile. Has external arrow icon (12.264px).

HeaderMain_navItemsRow.classes: Desktop: flex gap-8 items-start justify-end. aria-label='Main Navigation'.
HeaderMain_navItemsRow.notes: Horizontal row of nav items on desktop. gap-8 = 32px matches Figma.

HeaderMain_navItemsList.notes: Inner blocks wrapper for nav items. Uses useInnerBlocksProps.

HeaderMain_mobileToggle.notes: Hamburger/close toggle. Only on mobile. Hamburger: 27.648x15.36px (w-7 = 28px). Close: 17.305x17.425px. Uses menu-open: variant to swap icons.

HeaderMain_mobileMenuPanel.classes: header-collapsable md:hidden flex flex-col gap-8 w-full transition-[max-height,opacity] duration-500 ease-in-out overflow-hidden
HeaderMain_mobileMenuPanel.notes: Collapsible mobile menu. Hidden on desktop (md:hidden). On mobile, collapses to 0 height when menu is closed. Contains divider, nav items (vertical accordion), search, and footer CTA. gap-8 = 32px matches Figma.

HeaderMain_mobileDivider.notes: Horizontal divider line below logo+close row in mobile expanded state. mt-4 to separate from the logo row (16px gap).

HeaderMain_mobileNavList.classes: flex flex-col gap-8 w-full list-none p-0 m-0
HeaderMain_mobileNavList.notes: Vertical list of nav items in mobile menu. gap-8 = 32px matches Figma. Each item stretches full width.

HeaderMain_mobileFooterSection.notes: Mobile menu footer with descriptive text + CTA button. gap-4 = 16px matches Figma. Only rendered when mobileCtaText or mobileCtaLink are set.

children.text: font-sans font-medium text-base leading-relaxed text-white (Roboto Medium 16px/1.5)
children.button: ThemeButton with custom white pill styling: bg-white text-charcoal border-white rounded-full p-4 font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] uppercase no-underline!

HeaderMainItem_wrapper.notes: Nav item wrapper. Contains the link/button and optional submenu dropdown.

HeaderMainItem_linkButton.element: button (when has children) or uses Link component (when no children)
HeaderMainItem_linkButton.classes: flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer bg-transparent border-0 p-0 font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase no-underline! transition-colors
HeaderMainItem_linkButton.notes: On desktop: horizontal flex with text + chevron. On mobile: full-width flex with justify-between. Items WITHOUT children use Link component directly. Items WITH children use a button element that toggles submenu.

HeaderMainItem_chevron.notes: Chevron icon (12.595x7.125px). Points down by default. Rotates 180deg when submenu is open. Only rendered when item has sub-items.

HeaderMainItem_submenuDropdownDesktop.classes: hidden md:block absolute top-full mt-4 bg-charcoal rounded-xl p-6 min-w-max z-50
HeaderMainItem_submenuDropdownDesktop.positionTry: Use CSS anchor positioning with @position-try fallbacks to prevent viewport overflow. If positioned left and overflows right, flip to right-aligned, and vice versa.
HeaderMainItem_submenuDropdownDesktop.notes: Desktop submenu dropdown. Absolute positioned below the nav bar (not below the item -- below the entire nav bar for visual effect). bg: charcoal (#1f1f1d close enough to #1e1e1c). rounded-xl = 12px. p-6 = 24px. Width adapts to content (auto). Visible only when submenu is open. Hidden completely on mobile (submenus expand inline on mobile).

tokenMapping.background: bg-charcoal (theme token, Figma: #1f1f1d, token: rgba(31,31,29,1) = #1f1f1d -- EXACT match)

HeaderMainItem_submenuListDesktop.classes: flex flex-col gap-4 list-none p-0 m-0
HeaderMainItem_submenuListDesktop.notes: Desktop submenu list. gap-4 = 16px matches Figma.

HeaderMainItem_submenuListMobile.classes: flex flex-col gap-1 list-none p-0 m-0 pl-6
HeaderMainItem_submenuListMobile.notes: Mobile submenu list (inline accordion). gap-1 = 4px matches Figma. pl-6 = 24px left indent matches Figma.

HeaderMainSubItem_link.classes: Desktop: py-2 font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase no-underline! hover:text-neon-green transition-colors block. Mobile: py-2 font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase no-underline! hover:text-neon-green transition-colors block
HeaderMainSubItem_link.notes: Submenu link item. py-2 = 8px matches Figma. Font: the Figma shows 'Roboto Medium' but the theme uses 'General Sans' as font-sans -- we should use font-sans (General Sans) to match the project convention since General Sans and Roboto are similar. Uppercase.

behavior.layout: Vertical stack: SEP pill button on top, nav bar below with 8px gap
behavior.navBar: Shows logo (26px tall) and hamburger toggle
behavior.menuExpanded: Nav bar expands to show: divider, vertical nav items (accordion with inline submenus), search (full-width), mobile CTA footer
behavior.submenus: Expand inline as accordion below parent item with 24px left indent. Active item text turns neon-green, chevron rotates 180deg.
behavior.sepLink: Rendered as separate pill button above the nav bar
behavior.search: Full-width inside menu panel with pb-2 (8px)
behavior.mobileFooter: Text + Contact Us button at bottom of menu panel

tablet.behavior: Same as desktop. The md: breakpoint (768px) is where the switch occurs. At 768px+ the full desktop nav bar is shown.

behavior.layout: Single rounded bar with logo left, right column (utility row + nav items) right
behavior.navBar: Full bar: logo (57px tall), utility row (search 250px + separator + SEP link), nav items row (horizontal with 32px gap)
behavior.submenus: Absolute dropdown panels below nav bar. Auto-width based on content. Alignment left/right per backend setting, with CSS position-try fallback for viewport overflow prevention.
behavior.sepLink: Inline text link with external arrow in utility row
behavior.search: 250px wide in utility row with pb-1 (4px)

emptyState.sepLink: When sepLink.url is empty: hide SEP mobile button, hide SEP desktop link, hide vertical separator
emptyState.searchLink: When searchLink.url is empty: hide search link on both desktop and mobile
emptyState.mobileCtaText: When empty: hide the mobile footer text
emptyState.mobileCtaLink: When mobileCtaLink.url is empty: hide the mobile CTA button
emptyState.mobileFooterSection: When both mobileCtaText and mobileCtaLink are empty: hide entire mobile footer section
emptyState.navItems: When no inner blocks: hide hamburger toggle, hide mobile menu panel, nav items area is empty
emptyState.submenuItems: When HeaderMainItem has no HeaderMainSubItem children: hide chevron, render as Link instead of button

editorBehavior.blockNaming: renameBlock() on HeaderMainItem and HeaderMainSubItem to sync with link title
editorBehavior.scrollPreview: useEffect with iframe scroll detection to preview fixed/scroll behavior in editor
editorBehavior.mobilePreview: useIframeIsMobile to show mobile layout when editor is narrow

desktop.toggle: Click on nav item button opens/closes submenu dropdown
desktop.closeOnEscape: Escape key closes active submenu and returns focus to trigger button
desktop.onlyOneOpen: Only one submenu can be open at a time. Opening one closes others.
desktop.keyboardNav: ArrowDown/ArrowUp to navigate submenu items. Tab closes submenu.

mobile.toggle: Click on nav item toggles inline accordion expansion
mobile.accordion: Multiple submenus can be open at a time (independent accordions)
mobile.activeState: Active (expanded) item text turns neon-green, chevron rotates 180deg

scrollBehavior.description: Per section 3 of header.md block-type documentation
scrollBehavior.implementation: Follow sections 3.1 through 4.7 of header.md exactly

navBar.element: The main nav items container is a <nav> with aria-label='Main Navigation'

focusTrapping.mobileMenu: Tab wraps within mobile menu. Escape closes menu and returns focus to toggle.
focusTrapping.desktopSubmenu: ArrowDown/Up navigate items. Escape closes and returns focus to trigger. Tab closes submenu.

accessibility.inertAttribute: Applied to mobile menu focusable elements when menu is closed. Removed when open or on desktop.

statesFile.reason: Needed for collapsed mobile menu state (not-menu-open:max-h-0, etc.). Referenced in header.md block-type doc but not yet defined in this project.

MainMenuOpen.description: Hamburger menu icon (3 horizontal lines). ~27.648x15.36px. White color.
MainMenuOpen.notes: Create as simple SVG with 3 horizontal lines in a 24x24 or native viewBox.

ChevronDown.description: Down-pointing chevron for nav items with submenus. 12.595x7.125px. White color (turns neon-green when active).
ChevronDown.notes: Use currentColor for fill so it inherits text color. Rotated 180deg via CSS when active.

ExternalArrow.description: Diagonal arrow for external links. 12.264x12.264px. White color.
ExternalArrow.notes: Arrow pointing top-right to indicate external link.

BlockIcons.HeaderMain.svg: 24x24 SVG representing the main nav section

colors.notes: Figma shows #1e1e1c, theme has charcoal at #1f1f1d. Use bg-charcoal.

typography.notes: Figma uses Roboto but project uses General Sans (font-sans). Both are similar sans-serif, General Sans is the project font. Use font-sans.
Same Roboto to General Sans substitution.
Roboto to General Sans. leading-relaxed is 1.625, Figma is 1.5. Use leading-[1.5] for exact match.

decisions.question: Should the header use a multi-section structure (HeaderQuickAccess + HeaderMain) or a single section?
decisions.decision: Single section (HeaderMain only). The design shows one unified rounded bar containing everything -- logo, utility row, and nav items. There is no separate secondary nav bar.
decisions.evidence: Figma node 785:34245 shows a single Navigation element containing all content. No separate quick access bar.
decisions.rationale: The design unifies all navigation elements into one charcoal rounded bar. A separate HeaderQuickAccess section would introduce unnecessary complexity.

decisions.question: Should the SEP (Student Engagement Platform) link be a separate block or an attribute?
decisions.decision: Attribute of HeaderMain (sepLink object). It renders differently on desktop (text link) vs mobile (pill button) but is the same content.
decisions.evidence: Figma shows the same 'Student Engagement Platform' text in both mobile pill button and desktop utility row. Spec says 'do not duplicate elements'.
decisions.rationale: Single attribute, two render paths based on breakpoint. Avoids HTML duplication per spec instruction.

decisions.question: Should the search be a form input or a styled link?
decisions.decision: Styled link (not a form). It's a decorative element linking to a search page.
decisions.evidence: Figma shows a static 'SEARCH' text with icon, no input field interaction. The element is likely a link to /search/ or similar.
decisions.rationale: A real search form would need a form element, input, and submit handling. The Figma design shows a link styled to look like a search input.

decisions.question: Figma uses 'Roboto Medium' for submenu items, SEP link, and CTA text, but the project uses 'General Sans' as its sans-serif font. Which to use?
decisions.decision: Use General Sans (font-sans) for all text. The project has no Roboto font defined in its theme tokens.
decisions.evidence: typography.css defines only font-sans (General Sans) and font-heading (Anton). No Roboto token exists.
decisions.rationale: Consistency with the project's design system. General Sans and Roboto are similar enough that the visual difference is negligible.

decisions.question: Nav bar background is #1e1e1c in Figma, but the theme charcoal token is #1f1f1d. Which to use?
decisions.decision: Use bg-charcoal (#1f1f1d). The 1-shade difference is imperceptible.
decisions.evidence: colors.css: --color-charcoal: rgba(31, 31, 29, 1) = #1f1f1d. Figma: #1e1e1c. Delta: 1 in R/G channels.
decisions.rationale: Using the theme token ensures consistency. The difference is not visible to human eyes.

decisions.question: How should submenu dropdowns handle viewport overflow?
decisions.decision: Use CSS anchor positioning with @position-try fallbacks. Also provide a backend option (submenuAlignment attribute) for manual left/right alignment per nav item.
decisions.evidence: Spec explicitly states: 'When opening the submenu items, besides having the option on the backend to align them to the left or right of the parent, also use position-try to make it not open outside of the viewport.'

decisions.question: Where is the submenu dropdown positioned -- below the nav item or below the entire nav bar?
decisions.decision: Below the entire nav bar, offset horizontally to align with the parent nav item.
decisions.evidence: Figma node 1417:2397 shows the 'About' submenu dropdown positioned at top: 328px, well below the nav bar bottom edge (~222px). The dropdown is offset from the nav bar, not flush with the item.
decisions.rationale: The design shows a clear gap between the nav bar bottom and the submenu top. The dropdown floats below the rounded nav bar.

decisions.question: Should the mobile menu panel be a separate section that slides in, or should it expand within the nav bar?
decisions.decision: Expand within the nav bar. The mobile closed state shows a compact bar with logo+hamburger. The open state shows the bar expanding to include all content.
decisions.evidence: Figma node 811:61228 shows the mobile open state is still within the same rounded bar (bg: #1e1e1c, rounded: 25px). The bar grows vertically to contain all content. It's not a separate overlay.
decisions.rationale: The rounded corners and background are consistent between closed and open states -- it's the same element expanding.

decisions.question: How to handle the not-menu-open variant for collapsed mobile state?
decisions.decision: Add a new @custom-variant not-menu-open to states.css. Use CSS :not(.menu-open) selector.
decisions.evidence: states.css already defines menu-open variant. The block-type doc references not-menu-open: pattern. Need the inverse variant.
decisions.rationale: Following existing pattern conventions in the project.

decisions.question: How should the mobile CTA button be styled -- should it use ThemeButton or custom styling?
decisions.decision: Custom styling matching the Figma design. The mobile CTA button (Contact Us) is white bg, charcoal text, pill-shaped, which matches the project's secondary button in dark mode but with specific styling. Use a custom class set rather than btn-secondary to avoid the gradient hover animations which would look wrong for this simple white button.
decisions.evidence: Figma shows: bg: #ffffff, border: 1px solid #ffffff, rounded: 119.02px (pill), p: 16px, Roboto Medium 16px/1.16, #1f1f1d text, uppercase. This is simpler than the btn-secondary gradient animation.
decisions.rationale: The btn-secondary class has gradient hover animations that don't match this simple white pill button in the mobile menu.

decisions.question: Should mobile accordion submenus allow multiple open or only one at a time?
decisions.decision: Allow multiple submenus open at a time (independent accordions).
decisions.evidence: Figma mobile open state only shows one submenu expanded (About), but this is a static mockup. Standard accordion UX in mobile menus allows multiple open for better navigation.
decisions.rationale: More usable for mobile users who may want to compare submenu options. Each item controls its own state independently.

decisions.question: Should the nav items that DON'T have submenus (e.g., 'Student Voices') render as links or buttons?
decisions.decision: Render as Link component (anchor element). Only items WITH sub-items render as buttons (to toggle submenu).
decisions.evidence: Figma shows 'Student Voices' without a chevron icon, indicating it has no submenu and is a direct navigation link.
decisions.rationale: Semantic correctness -- items that navigate should be links, items that toggle should be buttons.

testPage.title: TEST: Header

variations.name: Full Content
variations.description: All fields populated with realistic content. SEP link, search, multiple nav items with submenus, mobile CTA.

HeaderMain.mobileCtaText: Have an idea for a service or way we can better serve you?

variations.name: Minimal Content
variations.description: Only logo and a few nav items, no SEP link, no search, no mobile CTA.

variations.name: Hide on Scroll Down (Desktop)
variations.description: Tests the hide-on-scroll-down behavior on desktop.

variations.name: Hide on Scroll Down (Both)
variations.description: Tests hide-on-scroll-down on both desktop and mobile.

variations.name: Many Nav Items (Overflow)
variations.description: Tests horizontal overflow with many nav items.

variations.name: Long Submenu Items
variations.description: Tests submenu dropdown with long item text to verify auto-width.

link.title: Comprehensive Health and Dental Insurance Overview for Students
Understanding Your Coverage Benefits and Exclusions
How to Opt Into Available Plans During Open Enrollment

notes: Test mobile menu by resizing browser to < 768px
Test submenu keyboard navigation (Tab, Arrow keys, Escape)
Test scroll behavior by adding enough content below the header to scroll
Test focus trapping in mobile menu (Tab should wrap, Escape should close)
Verify submenu position-try prevents viewport overflow by opening right-aligned submenus near right edge
Verify inert attribute is applied to mobile menu items when menu is closed
Verify logo links to home page and opens correctly

implementationNotes.singleHtmlNoduplication: CRITICAL: The spec says 'do not duplicate elements'. The SEP link, search, and nav items must be rendered ONCE in the HTML and use CSS to change their presentation between desktop and mobile. The SEP link on mobile is a SEPARATE element (pill button above the nav bar) from the desktop inline text link -- this is the one exception where the same CONTENT appears in two places but they are structurally different elements (one is above the nav bar, one is inside it). Since they can't be the same DOM element in two positions, this requires TWO render outputs but driven by ONE attribute.
implementationNotes.submenuPositioning: Desktop submenu dropdowns should use CSS anchor positioning. The submenu container is positioned absolute relative to the nav items row (or the nav bar). Use position-anchor to link the dropdown to its trigger button. Define @position-try fallbacks: if the default alignment causes overflow, try the opposite side. Fallback: if CSS anchor positioning is not supported, fall back to simple absolute positioning with left-0 or right-0.
implementationNotes.mobileMenuExpansion: The mobile menu expands WITHIN the rounded nav bar. The nav bar element grows vertically. Use max-height transition (from max-h-0 to max-h-[2000px]) for smooth animation. The not-menu-open variant controls the collapsed state.
implementationNotes.fontSubstitution: All text uses font-sans (General Sans Medium) regardless of whether Figma shows Roboto. This is a project-wide convention.
implementationNotes.scrollBehaviorJS: Follow sections 3-4 of the header.md block-type documentation exactly for scroll behavior implementation. The HeaderMain.js file handles start height calculation, fixed offset, scroll direction detection, and height tracking.
implementationNotes.editorScrollPreview: Follow section 5 of header.md for the editor useEffect that previews scroll behavior inside the block editor's iframe.
implementationNotes.zIndexStrategy: Since there is only one section (HeaderMain), z-150 is sufficient. No z-200 needed (no separate HeaderQuickAccess).
implementationNotes.containerClass: The nav bar uses its own padding (px-8 py-4) with rounded-[25px]. Inside, the content is NOT wrapped in a .container class -- the nav bar IS the full-width container with its own padding. On mobile, the nav bar similarly uses px-8 py-4.
implementationNotes.noContainerDiv: Unlike typical blocks that use a div.container wrapper, this header does NOT use the theme's .container class. The nav bar provides its own contained layout via px-8 (32px padding). This ensures the rounded corners appear at the edges.
